name: Cinnamon
summary: A warming spice studied for a modest effect on blood sugar — though the trials are small and their results conflict.

benefits.mechanism: Compounds in cinnamon appear to slow stomach emptying and improve how cells respond to insulin. A meta-analysis in type 2 diabetes found cinnamon lowered fasting blood glucose — but the authors stress the trials were small and their results conflicting, so treat the effect as modest and unproven.

citations.title: Cinnamon use in type 2 diabetes: an updated systematic review and meta-analysis (Allen et al., Ann Fam Med 2013)

surprisingFacts.text: Most supermarket cinnamon is cassia, which is high in coumarin — a compound that can stress the liver in large daily doses. 'True' Ceylon cinnamon contains far less, so it's the safer choice if you use a lot.

cautions.effect: Everyday cassia cinnamon — the supermarket default — contains coumarin, which can strain the liver at high regular intakes. You would not feel it day to day, which is exactly why it is worth knowing.
cautions.threshold: A few teaspoons of cassia daily approaches Europe's tolerable daily intake for coumarin. Ceylon ("true") cinnamon has far less.

citations.title: Cassia cinnamon as a source of coumarin in cinnamon-flavored food and food supplements in the United States (Wang et al., J Agric Food Chem 2013)
